name: Climb to the Sollube antennas and to the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve - circular from Bermeo
name_original: Subida a las antenas de Sollube y a la Reserva de la Biosfera de Urdaibai — circular desde Bermeo
description: This difficult 23.6-mile road cycling route from Bermeo climbs to the Sollube antennas, offering panoramic views over the Urdaibai Biosphere
region_intro: Road cycling around Bermeo offers diverse terrain within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, characterized by a dramatic coastline, rugged cliffs, and the basin of the Oka river. The region features rolling green hills, lush forests, and the prominent Sollube Mountain, providing varied elevation changes. This combination of coastal and mountain landscapes creates a network of routes suitable for different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Bermeo The most popular road cycling route is Climb to the Sollube antennas and to the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve - circular from Bermeo, a 23.6 miles (37.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends Sollube Mountain, offering views over the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Laida Beach – Arteaga Castle loop from Bermeo, a moderate 31.5 miles (50.6 km) path. This route provides coastal views and passes by the Mundaka estuary. Local road cyclists also love the Matxitxako Viewpoint – Jata Climb loop from Bermeo, a 35.5 miles (57.1 km) trail leading through the dramatic coastline and featuring the Jata climb, often completed in about 3 hours 29 minutes. Road cycling around Bermeo is defined by its dramatic coastline, the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, and the varied topography of Sollube Mountain. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging rides. The routes in Bermeo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Bermeo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Bermeo?
A: Road cycling around Bermeo offers a diverse mix of terrain within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. You'll encounter dramatic coastlines, rugged cliffs, and the basin of the Oka river. The region features rolling green hills, lush forests, and the prominent Sollube Mountain, providing varied elevation changes for both coastal and mountain cycling experiences.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride in Bermeo?
A: Yes, Bermeo offers routes for all levels, including easier options. While many routes feature significant climbs, there are paths with elevation differences between 200 and 800 meters. These typically follow flatter sections, such as routes along the Oka river towards the Mundaka estuary, providing a more relaxed cycling experience.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Bermeo?
A: For experienced cyclists, Bermeo offers demanding routes that tackle significant climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032132665/climb-to-the-sollube-antennas-and-to-the-urdaibai-biosphere-reserve-circular-from-bermeo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the Sollube antennas and to the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve - circular from Bermeo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 37.9 km route with over 1100 meters of elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15057829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matxitxako Viewpoint – Jata Climb loop from Bermeo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 57.1 km and includes the demanding Jata climb, with nearly 1500 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Bermeo?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Bermeo are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032132665/climb-to-the-sollube-antennas-and-to-the-urdaibai-biosphere-reserve-circular-from-bermeo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the Sollube antennas and to the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve - circular from Bermeo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24716754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laida Beach – Arteaga Castle loop from Bermeo&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied scenery and return you to your starting point.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes in Bermeo?
A: Road cycling routes in Bermeo often pass by stunning natural features and landmarks. You can expect views of the dramatic coastline, the Mundaka estuary, and the Urdaibai marsh. Notable attractions include the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/275684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint John of Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/527262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matxitxako Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, and various viewpoints offering panoramic vistas over the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views over the Urdaibai marsh&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints worth visiting on a road bike around Bermeo?
A: Absolutely. The region is dotted with exceptional viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15057829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matxitxako Viewpoint – Jata Climb loop from Bermeo&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights the Matxitxako Viewpoint, offering incredible vistas where the blue of the sea merges with the green of the cliffs. Other routes provide access to scenic spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4006662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from San Pedro de Atxarre&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4687497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talaia (276 m)&lt;/a&gt;, showcasing the beauty of the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Bermeo?
A: The road cycling routes in Bermeo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars. Cyclists often praise the spectacular scenery, the well-paved roads, and the variety of routes that cater to different fitness levels, from coastal views to challenging mountain climbs.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through charming towns or villages near Bermeo?
A: Yes, many routes are designed to take you through charming villages and towns, allowing you to experience the local culture. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24716715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mundaka Harbor and Surf Spot – Muxika town centre loop from Bermeo&lt;/a&gt; will lead you through Mundaka, famous for its surf spot and picturesque harbor, and further inland to Muxika.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Bermeo?
A: The Basque Country, including Bermeo, generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes, ideal for exploring. Summer can be warm but is also popular, especially for coastal rides. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there options for shorter road cycling routes in Bermeo?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a shorter ride, Bermeo has options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31899789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matxitxako Lighthouse – Matxitxako Viewpoint loop from Bermeo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16.9 km route, perfect for a quicker spin while still offering scenic coastal views and a visit to the iconic lighthouse.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Bermeo on komoot?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes available around Bermeo on komoot, offering a wide range of distances and difficulty levels to suit every rider. These include 1 easy route, 9 moderate routes, and 8 difficult routes, ensuring plenty of options for your next adventure.
tips: Narrow lanes up to the top of Sollube. It’s a dead end but nice view of the surrounding land & mountains almost 360 degrees at the top.

Sollube from Bermeo is a climb in the Basque Country region. It has a length of 8.2 kilometers and an ascent of 651 vertical meters with a slope of 7.9% on average. The top of the ascent is located at 658 meters above sea level.

A town with a beach and mountains, and a wide network of trails for hiking in an exceptional natural setting. It is located on the banks of the Mundaka-Gernika estuary, a beautiful municipality with just over 1,600 inhabitants distributed in five neighbourhoods.
More info at: https://turismourdaibai.com/es/busturia/

A pretty fishing village of great tourist interest, it is known worldwide for being an ideal and unique place for surfing. You can get lost in its narrow streets, enjoy the marine atmosphere of the port, lose your gaze in the fantastic views from Portuondo or from the Atalaya viewpoint. You can also enjoy a relaxed walk to the Santa Catalina hermitage and enjoy this place of great beauty.
More info at: https://turismourdaibai.com/es/mundaka/

Bermeo has always been recognized as one of the most important inshore fishing ports on the Cantabrian Coast. We can find written documentation about Bermeo from the 11th century, but the date of its foundation is from the 13th century. Between the surprising viewpoint of Baztarre and the viewpoint of Torrontero, we can enjoy a curious mural that represents the landscape of the town before it was built as a villa.
More info at: https://turismourdaibai.com/es/bermeo/

faqs.question: Is this a challenging road cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as a "difficult" road ride, requiring very good fitness. It covers approximately 37.9 km with a substantial elevation gain of 1,110 meters, primarily from the ascent to Sollube.

faqs.question: What are the key climbs on this route?
faqs.answer: The most significant climb is to Sollube Summit Antennas and Viewpoint. The ascent from Bermeo to Sollube is about 8.2 kilometers long with an average gradient of 7.9% and a total ascent of 651 vertical meters, reaching a peak at 658 meters above sea level. Some sections feature gradients up to 15%, with ramps nearing 20%.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning scenery throughout. From the top of Sollube, you can enjoy almost 360-degree panoramic views of the surrounding Basque Country. The trail also passes through the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, providing diverse landscapes from coastal views near Mundaka Harbor and Surf Spot to green meadows and craggy mountains.

faqs.question: Where can I park in Bermeo to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As the route is circular from Bermeo Harbor and Historic Center, you can typically find parking within the town. Look for public parking areas near the harbor or the historic center, keeping in mind that availability might vary, especially during peak season.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers beautiful scenery year-round, but spring and autumn are generally ideal for cycling in the Basque Country, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can also be good, but be prepared for potentially warmer weather on the climbs. Winter cycling is possible but may involve colder temperatures and a higher chance of rain.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the way?
faqs.answer: Starting and ending in Bermeo, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. The route also passes through other settlements like Busturia and Mundaka within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, where you can find options for refreshments.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so dogs are generally not suitable companions for the ride itself. If you are visiting the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, specific regulations regarding dogs may apply in certain areas, especially protected natural zones. Always keep dogs on a leash and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required to cycle through the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. It is a UNESCO-designated area, and while you are free to explore, please respect the natural environment and any local regulations regarding protected areas.

faqs.question: What makes the Sollube climb on this route unique?
faqs.answer: This specific route from Bermeo to Sollube is considered by many to be the most enjoyable way to cycle this well-known climb, which has been featured in important cycling races for decades. A key advantage is the very low traffic compared to other routes to Sollube that use busier roads, offering a more tranquil and scenic experience.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the [CIMA BI05] Sollube * Bermeo, [CIMA BI05] Sollube * Almike, and Bizkaiko bira trails.

faqs.question: What is the road surface like on the climb to Sollube?
faqs.answer: The road surface is generally in good condition, making for a smooth ride. However, towards the very top of the climb to Sollube, it can become slightly broken in some parts. Despite this, it remains manageable for road bikes.

waypoints.name: Busturia in the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve
